```json
{
  "summary": {
    "verdict": "BLOCK",
    "one_liner": "Classic TOCTOU race condition allows overselling; no transaction means partial failures corrupt state."
  },

  "critical_findings": [
    {
      "title": "Race condition: stock check and decrement are not atomic",
      "risk": "Two concurrent requests both read stock=1, both pass the check, both decrement — resulting in stock going negative and overselling inventory.",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "addToCart lines 5-9 (getStock → check → addItem → decrementStock)",
      "reproduction": "1. Product X has stock=1. 2. User A calls addToCart(A, X, 1) — reads stock=1, passes check. 3. Before A's decrementStock executes, User B calls addToCart(B, X, 1) — also reads stock=1, passes check. 4. Both decrement. Stock is now -1. Two users 'own' the same unit.",
      "fix": "Wrap the entire operation in a database transaction with SELECT FOR UPDATE (or equivalent row-level lock) on the product row. Read stock inside the lock so only one concurrent caller can proceed: `await db.transaction(async (tx) => { const stock = await tx.products.getStockForUpdate(productId); if (stock < quantity) throw new Error('Insufficient stock'); await tx.cart.addItem(userId, productId, quantity); await tx.products.decrementStock(productId, quantity); });`",
      "time_to_fix": "30 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Stock integrity — product should never be sold beyond available inventory."
    },
    {
      "title": "No transaction: partial failure leaves cart and stock out of sync",
      "risk": "If addItem succeeds but decrementStock fails (timeout, DB error, process crash), the user has the item in their cart but stock was never decremented — or vice versa if the order were reversed.",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "addToCart lines 8-9 (addItem then decrementStock are independent awaits)",
      "reproduction": "1. User calls addToCart. 2. db.cart.addItem succeeds. 3. db.products.decrementStock throws (connection dropped, constraint violation, timeout). 4. Cart now contains an item that stock doesn't reflect. No rollback occurs.",
      "fix": "Both operations must be inside a single database transaction so they commit or rollback atomically. This is solved by the same transaction wrapper recommended for finding #1.",
      "time_to_fix": "Included in finding #1 fix",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Data consistency — cart state and inventory state must always agree."
    },
    {
      "title": "No input validation on quantity allows zero, negative, or non-integer values",
      "risk": "A public API caller can pass quantity=0 (no-op that silently succeeds), quantity=-5 (increments stock instead of decrementing, effectively manufacturing inventory), or quantity=1.5 (fractional stock corruption).",
      "severity": "High",
      "location": "addToCart function signature — no validation before use",
      "reproduction": "Call addToCart('user1', 'prod1', -10). Stock check passes (any stock >= -10 is true). addItem adds a negative-quantity line. decrementStock subtracts -10, i.e., adds 10 to stock. Attacker has inflated inventory.",
      "fix": "Add validation at function entry: `if (!Number.isInteger(quantity) || quantity <= 0) throw new Error('Quantity must be a positive integer');` Also validate userId and productId are non-empty strings.",
      "time_to_fix": "5 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Input safety — public API must reject malformed input before any DB interaction."
    }
  ],

  "important_issues": [
    {
      "title": "No idempotency protection for duplicate requests",
      "description": "A network retry or double-click from the client will add the same item twice. Consider an idempotency key parameter to deduplicate within a time window.",
      "location": "addToCart function signature"
    },
    {
      "title": "Stock decrement happens after cart add — order should be reversed or atomic",
      "description": "Even within a transaction, decrementing stock first (reserve-then-add) is safer because it fails fast on contention rather than creating a cart entry that must be rolled back.",
      "location": "addToCart lines 8-9"
    }
  ],

  "suggestions": [
    "Consider using an optimistic concurrency approach (version column on product row) as an alternative to SELECT FOR UPDATE if lock contention becomes a bottleneck at scale.",
    "Add a stock reservation pattern with TTL for high-traffic products so cart additions don't permanently lock inventory."
  ],

  "production_readiness": {
    "deployment_risks": "Current code will oversell inventory under any concurrent load. The race window is wide (two separate round-trips between check and decrement).",
    "rollback_capability": "N/A — this is a function review, not a deployment.",
    "backward_compatibility": "Fix is backward compatible; function signature does not change (unless idempotency key is added).",
    "monitoring_coverage": "No monitoring mentioned. Should alert on negative stock values as a canary for race condition exploitation.",
    "breaking_changes": {
      "api_breaking": false,
      "db_destructive": false,
      "shared_library": false,
      "auth_security": false,
      "data_pipeline": false,
      "config_changes": false,
      "details": []
    },
    "safe_to_deploy": false,
    "rollback_plan": "Do not deploy until race condition and transaction issues are resolved. Existing data should be audited for negative stock values if this code has already been in production."
  },

  "escalations": [],

  "confidence": {
    "level": 97,
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "Race condition is trivially reproducible under concurrent load",
      "Partial failure scenario is a textbook non-transactional consistency bug",
      "Negative quantity exploit is verifiable by reading the code"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Whether db.products.decrementStock has its own internal guard against negative values (would mitigate but not fix the race)"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "db operations are standard async DB calls without internal locking or transactional semantics",
      "decrementStock performs a simple UPDATE SET stock = stock - quantity without a CHECK constraint"
    ]
  }
}
```

content.thinking:
Let me analyze this code for production risks.

Key hypotheses:
1. Race condition between stock check and decrement - classic TOCTOU
2. No transaction wrapping the operations - partial failure leaves inconsistent state
3. No input validation on quantity

Classification: `none` - these are code-level fixes (transactions, SELECT FOR UPDATE, validation).
